title: CSS
desc: Your page is bare. This is where it gets dressed. Not with a list of properties — but with understanding why the box is wider than you made it, and why your rule isn't being applied.

basics.title: Getting started
basics.desc: Where CSS lives and how you pick what to change. The first lesson gives you no property — it gives you an ugly page and the question of what to do about it.

box.title: The box
box.desc: Everything on a page is a box. You set the width to 300 and measure 340. This section explains why, and how to stop fixing it with mental arithmetic.

text.title: Text and colour
text.desc: Font, size, line height, colour. Plus a check most courses skip: can this actually be read, or only on your monitor.

cascade.title: The cascade
cascade.desc: You wrote the rule. It's correct. It isn't applied, and the browser says nothing. This is the most confusing part of CSS and I'm not going to pretend it's simple.

layout.title: Layout
layout.desc: Three boxes side by side. Try the obvious way — it won't work. Then come the tools built for exactly this.

components.title: Interface parts
components.desc: Links, lists, forms. You built them in the HTML course. Now you dress them — and find out why one field styles in a second while the dropdown next to it fights back.

responsive.title: Screens and states
responsive.desc: Works on your monitor. Broken on a phone. And one more thing: the mouse can see the button, the keyboard can't.

final.title: The finish
final.desc: An empty editor and one page. On your own.
